title: The better AI gets, the smaller its share of the economy might get – Alex Imas and Phil Trammell
summary: An economics discussion about how improved AI capabilities might paradoxically reduce AI's share of economic output, and explores questions of optimal taxation, wealth redistribution, and global inequality in an AGI-enabled economy. The hosts argue that counterintuitive economic principles apply to AI's role in future economies.
